mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to materialize in >70% of late-cycle expansion periods with rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during disinflation phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate creates a tightening real yield environment that compresses equity multiples and forces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive returns dominate when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP levels reflect sustained economic output growth that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or negative returns occur in >65% of high-GDP observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment exceeding 137,500 thousand persons confirms strong labor demand and wage growth potential, which boosts consumer confidence and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of rapid monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and weak demand, leading to earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns fail to materialize in >55% of post-shock recovery periods with falling INDPRO, or the mechanism shows no regime dependence in out-of-sample tests.
